hero.title: Train Like the Real Exam. Improve Like a Pro.
hero.description: Want a higher IELTS score? You don't need endless practice, you need smart practice. On this platform, you work with real exam-style tasks and get clear feedback powered by AI, so you always know what to improve.

choose.title: Choose
choose.description: Pick the practice module that matches your goal. Choose from full-length Academic reading or listening tests designed to reflect the latest IELTS exam format and difficulty.

complete.title: Complete
complete.description: Take the test in a focused, distraction-free environment that closely mirrors the real computer-based IELTS exam, including timing, layout, and question flow.

score.title: Get Score
score.description: Submit your answers and receive instant results. See your overall score, detailed breakdowns by question type, and clear explanations for correct and incorrect answers.

improve.title: Improve
improve.description: Get personalized AI suggestions based on your mistakes. Review targeted lessons, practice specific question types, and access recommended resources to strengthen your weak areas.

features.subtitle: Built for authentic IELTS Reading and Listening preparation with exam-focused workflows.

authentic.title: Authentic Interface

instructor.title: Personal instructor

one.name: Dilshod Karimov
one.text: Reading practice felt structured and realistic. I improved my time control in just a few mock attempts.

two.name: Madina Ismoilova
two.text: Listening sessions with countdowns helped me stay calm under pressure. My score became much more stable.

three.name: Rustam Gafurov
three.text: The interface is clean and close to the real exam format. Feedback after each attempt is very practical.

four.name: Shahnoza Tursunova
four.text: Progress tracking showed exactly which question types were slowing me down. It made my preparation focused.

five.name: Azizbek Rakhimov
five.text: The mock tests are consistent and professional. I now follow a daily routine without wasting time.

six.name: Akmaljon Rasulov
six.text: I liked the balanced mix of Reading and Listening tasks. It helped me prepare steadily every week.

guidelines.subtitle: Keep every published test unique and trackable.
guidelines.rule1: Never reuse a question signature across published tests. Duplicate variants must remain in draft until rewritten.

instructions.writingDefault: Write a focused IELTS response and keep your structure clear from introduction to conclusion.
instructions.speakingDefault: Record your answer clearly, then add short self-reflection notes about fluency and coherence.
instructions.questionSetDefault: Complete the assigned question set and show how you eliminated distractors in uncertain items.
instructions.writingTechEssay: Some people believe that technology improves communication, while others think it reduces real human interaction. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
instructions.writingTask1Report: Summarize the key trends in the chart. Select and report the main features and make relevant comparisons.
instructions.speakingMock: Speaking Part 2: Describe a person who inspired you to improve your academic goals.
instructions.speakingJournal: Record a short fluency journal entry about your learning progress this week and include one specific improvement.
instructions.readingMatchingHeadings: Match each paragraph with the best heading and justify why the other options are less accurate.
instructions.readingTfng: Answer True / False / Not Given items and explain which statements are directly supported, contradicted, or unsupported.
instructions.listeningSection4: Complete Section 4 answers and submit brief notes explaining where the distractors appeared in the audio.
instructions.listeningNoteCompletion: Fill the note-completion gaps using exact words from the recording and check spelling/plural forms.
instructions.vocabularyMixed: Finish the vocabulary check, then write a brief reflection on the collocations you still confuse.

teacherNotes.writingDefault: Focus on coherence and avoid repeating the same sentence pattern.
teacherNotes.speakingDefault: Prioritize natural delivery over speed. Keep your ideas connected.
teacherNotes.questionSetDefault: Accuracy first. Mark uncertain items and review instruction keywords before final submission.
teacherNotes.writingTechEssay: Focus on clear structure and strong supporting examples.
teacherNotes.writingTask1Report: Do not include personal opinion. Keep the overview concise and data-focused.
teacherNotes.speakingMock: Use one concrete story and extend each point with clear detail.
teacherNotes.speakingJournal: Track filler words and pauses. Aim for smoother transitions between ideas.
teacherNotes.readingMatchingHeadings: Identify the central idea first, then match the heading.
teacherNotes.readingTfng: Do not infer beyond the text. Mark Not Given only when evidence is absent.
teacherNotes.listeningSection4: Watch for signpost language that signals answer transitions.
teacherNotes.listeningNoteCompletion: Double-check spelling and plural endings before submission.
teacherNotes.vocabularyMixed: Prioritize academic collocations you often miss in reading passages.

coachNote.title: Coach Note
coachNote.description: Use these insights in your next focused practice session to convert feedback into measurable score improvement.

writingTech.assessment: Your essay demonstrates a good understanding of the prompt and maintains a clear position. The score is currently limited by repetitive grammatical framing and narrower lexical variation in body paragraphs. Strengthening transitions and expanding your word choice range can move this response toward Band 7.0.
writingTech.prompt: Some people believe that technology improves communication, while others think it reduces real human interaction. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
writingTech.preview: In the contemporary era, the rapid advancement of technology has fundamentally reshaped the way people communicate with each other. While digital tools improve speed and access, they can also reduce meaningful face-to-face interaction in daily life...
writingTech.fullSubmission:
In the contemporary era, the rapid advancement of technology has fundamentally reshaped the way people communicate with each other. While many people argue that communication has become more efficient and inclusive, others believe that these developments weaken the depth of social relationships. This essay discusses both perspectives before presenting my view.

On the one hand, technology offers clear communication advantages. Instant messaging, video conferencing and collaborative platforms allow people to stay connected across long distances. In academic and professional contexts, this speed can increase productivity and reduce delays in decision-making. For example, international teams can now coordinate projects in real time without needing to travel.

On the other hand, frequent digital communication may reduce the quality of interpersonal exchange. Many conversations become shorter and less emotionally expressive when people rely on text-based interaction. In addition, overuse of social media can create superficial connections that replace deeper in-person dialogue. As a result, people may feel connected online but isolated in everyday life.

In my opinion, technology itself is not the problem; the issue lies in how it is used. If individuals balance digital convenience with intentional face-to-face communication, technology can strengthen rather than weaken human relationships.

In conclusion, technology improves access and efficiency in communication, but it can also reduce social depth when overused. A balanced approach is necessary to gain benefits without losing genuine interpersonal connection.
writingTech.criteriaNote: Criteria based on standard IELTS Writing band descriptors.

introThesis.title: Introduction & Thesis
introThesis.feedback: Your paraphrase is clear and the thesis establishes both sides of the discussion effectively. The opening is relevant and coherent, with a clear task focus.
introThesis.highlight: Use one stronger academic synonym in the first two sentences to increase lexical range immediately.

bodyPositive.title: Body Paragraph 1: Positive Impacts
bodyPositive.feedback: The argument about communication efficiency is logical and the international-team example supports your point well. The paragraph progression is smooth.
bodyPositive.highlight: Add one sentence on social inclusion (e.g., rural or remote access) to improve depth.

bodyNegative.title: Body Paragraph 2: Negative Impacts
bodyNegative.feedback: You identify a relevant downside and maintain topic focus. The comparison between online connection and isolation is clear and persuasive.
bodyNegative.highlight: Avoid repeating basic structures like "can + verb". Add one complex sentence with subordination.

conclusion.title: Conclusion
conclusion.feedback: The conclusion is concise, balanced, and directly aligned with your position. It summarizes key points without introducing new ideas.

vocabularyGrammar.title: Vocabulary & Grammar Notes
vocabularyGrammar.feedback: Vocabulary is generally accurate, but some lexical choices are repeated across paragraphs. Grammar control is stable for simple structures, while complex variation is still limited.
vocabularyGrammar.highlight: In your next draft, replace at least 4 repeated high-frequency words with precise academic alternatives.

practiceSimilarTask.title: Practice Similar Task
practiceSimilarTask.description: Reattempt a discussion essay with balanced argument structure.

reviewMistakes.title: Review Mistakes
reviewMistakes.description: Revisit your recent weak areas and error patterns.

openAiCoach.title: Open AI Coach
openAiCoach.description: Get a personalized next-session plan from AI Coach.

readingMatching.assessment: You apply the matching-headings process with improving control, especially in elimination steps. Accuracy is still affected when paraphrases are subtle. Continue building paraphrase recognition and decision speed.
readingMatching.prompt: Reattempt a Matching Headings set and highlight why each eliminated option is incorrect.
readingMatching.preview: For Paragraph A I selected heading iv because the paragraph focuses on long-term ecological impact rather than short-term industrial growth...
readingMatching.fullSubmission:
Paragraph A - Heading iv: The paragraph explains long-term ecological effects and contrasts them with short-term gains.
Paragraph B - Heading i: This paragraph focuses on policy shifts and implementation barriers.
Paragraph C - Heading vi: Main idea is public resistance despite scientific consensus.
Elimination note: I rejected heading iii because it was too specific and referenced a detail that appears only once.
readingMatching.criteriaNote: Reading feedback criteria are aligned to task strategy and accuracy indicators.

instructionControl.title: Instruction Control
instructionControl.feedback: You follow task instructions accurately and maintain consistent answer format.

eliminationProcess.title: Elimination Process
eliminationProcess.feedback: Your elimination notes are clearer than before and show stronger rationale for rejecting distractors.
eliminationProcess.highlight: Write one short elimination reason immediately after each answer to reduce second-guessing.

paraphraseRecognition.title: Paraphrase Recognition
paraphraseRecognition.feedback: When wording changes are subtle, you still rely on surface keyword matching. Improve central-idea identification before option comparison.

runReadingDrill.title: Run Reading Drill
runReadingDrill.description: Practice a focused matching-headings speed set.

openCoachPlan.title: Open Coach Plan
openCoachPlan.description: Get a targeted paraphrase recognition plan.

chat.explainMoreLead: Let's deepen your plan: prioritize {weakestType}, then reinforce with {strongestType}.
chat.explainMoreStep1: 1. Keep a short error log after each practice set.
chat.explainMoreStep2: 2. Review only mistakes first, then retry without notes.
chat.explainMoreStep3: 3. End each session with one confidence drill in your strongest area.

sarah.expertise: Focuses on argument structure, lexical range, and band-lifting writing strategies.

david.expertise: Supports note-completion accuracy, fluency control, and high-pressure speaking confidence.

previews.sarahWriting: Please tighten your second body paragraph and add one stronger example.
previews.davidListening: Focus on signpost words before filling note-completion gaps.
previews.sarahReading: Your elimination logic improved. Keep practicing paraphrase detection.

messages.msg1: Great work on your last essay. Your thesis is clear, but paragraph two needs deeper support.
messages.msg2: Thank you. Should I focus on adding more academic examples or improving transitions first?
messages.msg3: Start with stronger examples. Then revise transitions in your final edit pass.
messages.msg4: I reviewed your Section 4 attempt. Your main issue is rushing after the first distractor.
messages.msg5: Understood. I will pause one second before writing each answer.
messages.msg6: Perfect. Also keep plural endings in mind for note completion.
messages.msg7: Your matching-headings score improved, but you still rely too much on direct keyword matching.
messages.msg8: Try identifying each paragraph's central idea before looking at options.
